title: Preparing the data
text: hbs_eu() returns data frame : row household. Expenditure variables follow COICOP naming convention (e.g. CP00 total expenditure, CP045 domestic energy, CP07 transport). Socioeconomic variables income quintiles, deciles, ventiles, percentiles computed national EU level. Gender-sensitive variables (gender reference person, feminization degree) included. country column identifies member state. full list variables available distributional analysis, see Available Variables.

title: Preparing the data
text: hbs_eu() accepts one year time. process multiple waves, call function per wave combine results: Intermediate files generated processing saved raw_data/inputs/ can reused subsequent sessions.
code: hbs_2010 <- hbs_eu(year = 2010, country = "all", path = "raw_data") hbs_2015 <- hbs_eu(year = 2015, country = "all", path = "raw_data") hbs_2020 <- hbs_eu(year = 2020, country = "all", path = "raw_data")

title: MEDUSA - Modelling Equity and DistribUtional impacts for Socioeconomic Analysis
text: Addressing critical challenges, climate change, requires ambitious policies promote social justice avoiding deepening existing inequalities, income gender disparities. achieve , policy impact assessments must take holistic view economy, energy, land, water systems also analyze distributional effects different population groups. Integrated Assessment Models (IAMs) play key role policy evaluation, often lack granularity needed capture socio-economic disparities. Micro-simulation models help bridge gap providing detailed, heterogeneous results allow policymakers identify vulnerable populations design targeted compensatory measures. However, despite growing emphasis distributional analysis academic literature, approaches yet widely integrated policymaking. key barrier lack accessible tools facilitate type analysis practitioners policymakers. MEDUSA first R package specifically designed fill gap. MEDUSA provides user-friendly framework conducting distributional analysis. simplifying complex modeling processes, enables broader audience —including researchers, policymakers, analysts— incorporate equity considerations policy design, ensuring policies effective socially just.

title: MEDUSA - Modelling Equity and DistribUtional impacts for Socioeconomic Analysis

Additionally, package combines calculation energy transport poverty indices. ❗❗ Note: moment, medusa package works Spain (INE microdata) EU countries (EUROSTAT microdata). package extended countries able provide raw data model.

title: Cleaned Household Budget Survey (HBS) microdata for 2006 — epf_list_2006
text: data structured named list following main components: - `epf_hg`: Household-level data including consumption expenditure (COICOP categories prefixed `EUR_`) key socioeconomic variables income groupings, household type, region, gender household reference person. - `epf_hgm`: Member-level data individual characteristics household members (e.g., age, gender, education). - `epf_hc`: Household-level data containing consumption quantities (physical amounts) COICOP category.

title: Cleaned Household Budget Survey (HBS) microdata for 2006 — epf_list_2006
text: - `QUINTILE`, `DECILE`, `VENTILE`, `PERCENTILE`: Income distribution indicators. - `REGION`: Autonomous Community residence (NUTS2). - `MUNISIZE`: Municipality size (`>10.000`, `<10.000`). - `ZONE`: Area type (`Urban`, `Semi-urban`, `Rural`). - `HHTYPE`: Household type (e.g., `Couples children`, `Single parent`, `Elderly alone`). - `CHILDREN`: Children household (`children`, `children`, `Large family`). - `POVERTY`: Poverty risk status (`risk`, `risk`). - `GENDERRP`: Gender reference person (`Man`, `Woman`). - `FEMDEGREE`: Feminization degree household (FD1 FD5). - `AGERP`: Age group reference person (`Young`, `Adult`, `Elder`). - `COUNTRYRP`: Country birth reference person (`Spain`, `EU27`, `Europe`, `Rest world`). - `STUDIESRP`: Education level (`Without studies` `Higher education`). - `PROFESSIONALSRP`: Professional status (`Employee`, `Self-employed`, `Employer`, ``, `apply`). - `CONTTYPERP`: Employment contract type (`Indefinite`, `Temporary`, `apply`). - `WORKDAYRP`: Working hours (`Full time`, `Part time`, `apply`). - `REGMR`: Tenure status main residence (`Ownership`, `Rented`, `Relinquish`).

title: Cleaned Household Budget Survey (HBS) microdata for 2014 — epf_list_2014
text: data structured named list following main components: - `epf_hg`: Household-level data including consumption expenditure (COICOP categories prefixed `EUR_`) key socioeconomic variables income groupings, household type, region, gender household reference person. - `epf_hgm`: Member-level data individual characteristics household members (e.g., age, gender, education). - `epf_hc`: Household-level data containing consumption quantities (physical amounts) COICOP category.

title: Cleaned Household Budget Survey (HBS) microdata for 2014 — epf_list_2014
text: - `QUINTILE`, `DECILE`, `VENTILE`, `PERCENTILE`: Income distribution indicators. - `REGION`: Autonomous Community residence (NUTS2). - `MUNISIZE`: Municipality size (`>10.000`, `<10.000`). - `ZONE`: Area type (`Urban`, `Semi-urban`, `Rural`). - `HHTYPE`: Household type (e.g., `Couples children`, `Single parent`, `Elderly alone`). - `CHILDREN`: Children household (`children`, `children`, `Large family`). - `POVERTY`: Poverty risk status (`risk`, `risk`). - `GENDERRP`: Gender reference person (`Man`, `Woman`). - `FEMDEGREE`: Feminization degree household (FD1 FD5). - `AGERP`: Age group reference person (`Young`, `Adult`, `Elder`). - `COUNTRYRP`: Country birth reference person (`Spain`, `EU27`, `Europe`, `Rest world`). - `STUDIESRP`: Education level (`Without studies` `Higher education`). - `PROFESSIONALSRP`: Professional status (`Employee`, `Self-employed`, `Employer`, ``, `apply`). - `CONTTYPERP`: Employment contract type (`Indefinite`, `Temporary`, `apply`). - `WORKDAYRP`: Working hours (`Full time`, `Part time`, `apply`). - `REGMR`: Tenure status main residence (`Ownership`, `Rented`, `Relinquish`).

title: medusa 0.2.0
text: New module supporting distributional, energy poverty, transport poverty analysis 27 EU countries using Eurostat Household Budget Survey (HBS) microdata (waves 2010, 2015 2020). hbs_eu(): Process Eurostat HBS microdata format required medusa (supports 27 EU countries, waves 2010, 2015 2020). calc_di_eu(): Calculate distributional impacts EU countries support two price shock scenarios, country-level disaggregation intersectional analysis. ex_shocks_eu(): Download two-scenario price shock template (columns CC_s1/CC_s2 per country). available_var_eu(): List variables available distributional analysis EU module. ex_var_intersec_eu(): Download full list 211 intersectional variable pairs available EU analysis. check_var_eu(): Validate requested variables present data. calc_ep_eu(): Calculate energy poverty indices country EU data. calc_tp_eu(): Calculate transport poverty indices country EU data.

title: medusa 0.1.0
text: First release medusa package Spain (INE microdata).

title: medusa 0.1.0
text: calc_di(): Calculate distributional impacts price shocks across socioeconomic groups (income deciles/quintiles, gender, household type, etc.). ex_shocks(): Download price shock template file. available_var_impact(): List variables available distributional analysis. ex_var_intersec(), available_var_intersec(): Manage intersectional variable pairs combined analysis (e.g. income x gender). calc_ep(): Calculate five energy poverty indices (10%, 2M, LIHC, HEP, HEP_LI). calc_tp(): Calculate four transport poverty indices (10%, 2M, LIHC, VTU).
